source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in Figure, a metal wire of mass $m$ slides frictionlessly on two rails. The rails are located in a vertical uniform magnetic field B. A constant current $I$ flows from generator G into the rails and back along the other rail. Find the velocity of the wire as a function of time $v(t)$. (At $t=0$, the wire is at rest.)

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'As shown in the figure'
the needed geometry/spacing parameter appears to come from the diagram rather than being fully specified in text
image_manifest shows 1 available image and registry_passed=true
multi_step: solving for v(t) requires combining electromagnetic force on the wire with Newton's second law
the problem includes multiple conditions to integrate: frictionless motion, uniform magnetic field, constant current, and initial condition v(0)=0
the target is a time-dependent expression rather than a direct lookup quantity
verifiability: answer_type=short_text with a specific symbolic expression
multiple-choice options are provided, so the expected result is closed-form and checkable
the derivation can be checked against standard physics relations and the initial condition

decision_reasons: question, answer, and required diagram are all present, so the core semantics are supported
registry_passed=true with no recorded asset issues
high verifiability_score (0.93) and high image_dependency_score (0.95) indicate a checkable image-based physics item
although some parameters are not fully specified in text, the referenced diagram is available, so this is not a blocking gap
